title: Polynomial-time 3-coloring of $P_t$-free graphs
statement_text: Is the 3-coloring problem solvable in polynomial time in the class of $P_t$-free graphs, for every fixed $t > 7$?
context_text: It is stated in the abstract and introduction as the central open problem motivating the paper: whether the 3-coloring problem can be solved in polynomial time in the class of graphs without an induced path on $t$ vertices, for fixed $t$. The known boundary is: polynomial-time for $P_7$-free graphs (Bonomo et al. [2]), and NP-complete for $k \geq 5$ colors on $P_6$-free graphs and for 4-coloring on $P_7$-free graphs (Huang [12]). The only unsettled complexity cases are $k=4, t=6$ and $k=3, t \geq 8$.
attributed_to: community open problem; surveyed in Golovach et al. [8]
paper_contribution: Provides a polynomial-time approximation algorithm that, given a 3-colorable $P_t$-free graph $G$, computes a coloring with at most $\max\!\left\{5,\, 2\left\lceil\tfrac{t-1}{2}\right\rceil - 2\right\}$ colors (or $\max\!\left\{4,\, \left\lceil\tfrac{t-1}{2}\right\rceil + 1\right\}$ colors if $G$ is triangle-free), running in time $O((3^{t-2}+t^2)|E(G)|+|V(G)|)$.
notes: No single originating author is cited for the open question; it is presented as a long-standing community problem. The paper source is truncated (text cuts off mid-proof of Lemma 10), so additional conjectures or open problems appearing later in the paper (e.g., near Theorem 12 or in a concluding section) may be missing from this extraction.
paper_title: Approximately coloring graphs without long induced paths
